Backoffice
Requirements Mapping page
View list of requriements mapping pairs
Do we need it for Process Requirements?
User with access to PICASso content in Backoffice should see new page "Requirements Mapping" under the "Product Requirements" section with the following content:
Filters:
Search field - allowing to search by Requirement Code
Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
Linked Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
Show Inactive - toggle button:
When unchecked (default) - only active mappings are visible in the list
When checked - both active and inactive mapping are shown
Table
with following columns with the mapping between requirements of different standard:
Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement
Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement
Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
Green checkmark - when active
Grey checkmark - when inactive
Actions - three dots menu button with following options:
Edit - allowing to edit the mapping pair which is already added
Activate (or Deactivate) - allowing to activate or deactivate the mapping pair depending on the current status of the pair.
Add/edit requirements mapping pair
On the "Requirements Mapping" page the user can add new requriements mapping pair or edit existing one by:
clicking the button "Add Requirements Mapping Pair" - "New Requirement Mapping Pair" page is opened.
clicking the menu button in the column "Actions" for the requirements mappig pair and selecting option "Edit"  - "Edit Requirement Mapping Pair" page is opened.
These pages should have the following fields:
Field
Data Type
Description
Standard
Dropdown, single select, mandatory
Allowing to select the standard of all the standards already available in the system
Requirement
Dropdown, multi select, mandatory
Activated after specific standard is selected, allowing to select requirements/sub-requirements which are related to this selected standard only.
Linked Standard
Dropdown with possibility to type, mandatory
Allowing to add new standard or select the name of all the standards already available in the system (if any were already created)
Linked Requirement
Dropdown, multi select, mandatory
Activated after specific standard is selected, allowing to select requirements/sub-requirements which are related to this selected linked standard only.
Is active
Checkbox
Allowing to update the status of requirements mapping pair from this screen:
When checked (default) - the mapping is considered as active
When unchecked - the mapping is considered inactive
After the data is entered, the user can click on the button "Save" and the system should validate if all the mandatory fields are filled in:
If at least 1 is empty - the user should see an error "Please fill in the required fields" at the top of the screen and corresponding empty field highlighted in red with error message "Required Field!".
If all are filled in - the user should see:
Success message at the top of the screen with the text "Requirement mapping pair is successfully added." or "Requirement mapping pair is successfully updated."
The user is redirected to the Requirements Mapping page.
And this new/updated mapping pair is updated in all the versions of the requirements selected in this mapping pair.
Note: if with edit previous requirements were removed - they should be removed from all the versions of requirements as well.
The user can also click on the "Back" button on the "New Requirement Mapping Pair" or "Edit Requirement Mapping Pair" page and no new mapping pair or no changes would be saved.
Activating/deactivating requirements mapping pair
If the requirements mapping pair is active, user can select the option "Deactivate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Requirements Mapping" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to deactivate?" and 2 options:
Cancel - closes the pop-up and requirements mapping pair remains active
Ok - closes the pop-up and deactivates the pair:
success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully deactivated".
status of the requirements mapping pair is updated to "Inactive".
mapping pair is not considered for the view in release, when specific view is selected for the release.
If the requirements mapping pair is inactive, user can select the option "Activate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Requirements Mapping" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to activate?" and 2 options:
Cancel - closes the pop-up and requirements mapping pair remains inactive
Ok - closes the pop-up and activates the pair:
success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully activated".
status of the requirements mapping pair is updated to "Active".
mapping pair is no considered for the view in release, when specific view is selected for the release.
Updating requirements mapping with the file upload
On the "Requirements Mapping" page the user should be able to download the list of the Requirements Mapping pairs available in the system by clicking the button "Download Requirements Mapping" and the file with the list of available mapping pairs would be exported with the following columns:
Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement (several values separated by comma)
Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement (several values separated by comma)
Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
True - when active
False - when inactive
The user should be also able to download empty template file by clicking the button "Template Download" and empty file with the same set of columns would be exported:
Standard (string)
Requirement (string, several values separated by comma)
Linked Standard (string)
Linked Requirement (string, several values separated by comma)
Is Active (boolean (True or False), empty value is considered as "True".
This downloaded template user can fill-in and upload back to the system using "Select file" button. Upon uploading the file - the following validations should be checked
Field
Validation
Error message
-
Wrong file format
Please upload valid file type (.xlsx)
-
File structure (set of the columns) does not correspond to the set of columns in the template file
Columns structure is incorrect, please use the columns from the template file.
Standard
Value is empty
Standard column can't be empty
Lines <line number>
Standard
Value specified does not correspond to any standards registered in the system
Standard does not exist in the system
Lines <line number>
Requirement
Value is empty
Requirement column can't be empty
Lines <line number>
Requirement
Value specified does not correspond to any requirement/sub-requriement registered in the system or existing requirements are divided by other symbol (not comma)
Requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma.
Lines <line number>
Linked Standard
Value is empty
Linked standard column can't be empty
Lines <line number>
Linked Standard
Value specified does not correspond to any standards registered in the system
Linked standard does not exist in the system
Lines <line number>
Linked Requirement
Value is empty
Linked requirement column can't be empty
Lines <line number>
Linked Requirement
Value specified does not correspond to any requirement/sub-requriement registered in the system or existing requirements are divided by other symbol (not comma)
Linked requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma.
Lines <line number>
Is Active
Invalid data is specified (other than "True", "False" or empty)
Is Active status contains unsupported value (can be only "True" or "False")
Lines <line number>
If all  the validations are successfulluy passed, the user should see:
Success message at the top of the screen with the text "File was uploaded successfully"
New/updated mapping pairs are updated in all the versions of the requirements selected in this mapping pair.
Note: if with edit previous requirements were removed - they should be removed from all the versions of requirements as well.
Requirements Mapping tab on the Edit Requirements page
In question if we need it
New tab "Requriements Mapping" should be added for the "Edit Product Requirement/Sub-requirement" page where all the standards and requirements linked with this requirement should be shown in the following content:
Filters:
Search field - allowing to search by Requirement Code
Standard - dropdown, allowing to select all standards available in the system
Show Inactive - toggle button:
When unchecked (default) - only active mappings are visible in the list
When checked - both active and inactive mapping are shown
Table
with following columns with the mapping between requirements of different standard:
Linked Standard - name of the standard, policy related to the requirement
Linked Requirement - codes of the requirement/sub-requirement
Note: value in this column is clickable and should redirect the user to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly.
Is Active - showing status of the mapping pair:
Green checkmark - when active
Grey checkmark - when inactive
"Standard" page for Product Requirements
View list of standards
User with access to PICASso content in Backoffice should see new page "Standards" under the "Product Requirements" section with the following content:
Filters:
Search field - allowing to search by Standard Name/Code
Show Inactive - toggle button:
When unchecked (default) - only active standards are visible in the list
When checked - both active and inactive standards are shown
Table
with following columns :
Code - unique code of the standard
Standard - name of the standard/policy related to the requirement
Note: the value in this column is clickable and opens "Edit Standard" page
Is Active - showing status of standard:
Green checkmark - when active
Grey checkmark - when inactive
Actions - three dots menu button with following options:
Edit - allowing to edit standard which is already added
Activate (or Deactivate) - allowing to activate or deactivate stadard depending on its current status.
Add/edit standard
On the "Standards" page the user can add new standard or edit existing one by:
clicking the button "Add Standard" - "New Standard" page is opened.
clicking on the standard's name or clicking the menu button in the column "Actions" and selecting option "Edit"  - "Edit Standard" page is opened.
These pages should have the following fields:
Field
Data Type
Description
Code
String, mandatory
Allowing to type unique code for the standard
Standard
String, mandatory
Allowing to type name of the standard that would be shown for configurations
Is active
Checkbox
Allowing to update the status of standard from this screen:
When checked (default) - standard is considered as active
When unchecked - standard is considered inactive
After the data is entered, the user can click on the button "Save" and the system should validate the following points:
If specified Code is already used for other standard already created - the user should see an error "Specified Code is already used for other standard. Please check and update the value" at the top of the screen and corresponding Code field highlighted in red with error message "Code should be unique".
If at least 1 mandatory field is empty - the user should see an error "Please fill in the required fields" at the top of the screen and corresponding empty field highlighted in red with error message "Required Field!".
If above validations are passed - the user should see:
Success message at the top of the screen with the text "Standard is successfully added." or "Standard is successfully updated."
The user is redirected to the Standards page.
Corresponding standard is updated for the Sources page right after the change is saved.
The user can also click on the "Back" button on the "New Standard" or "Edit Standard" page and no new standard or no changes would be saved.
Activating/deactivating standard
If the standard is active, user can select the option "Deactivate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Standards" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to deactivate?" and 2 options:
Cancel - closes the pop-up and standard remains active
Ok - closes the pop-up and deactivates the standard:
success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Standard <Standard name> is successfully deactivated".
status of the standard is updated to "Inactive".
standard cannot be selected for the Sources when they are edited.
If the standard is inactive, user can select the option "Activate" from the menu in the "Actions" column on the "Standards" page, and the confirmation pop-up would be shown to the user with the following text: "Are you sure you want to activate?" and 2 options:
Cancel - closes the pop-up and standard remains inactive
Ok - closes the pop-up and activates the pair:
success message is shown at the top of the screen: "Standard <Standard name> is successfully activated".
status of the standard is updated to "Active".
standard can be selected for the Sources when they are edited.
Add "Standard" field for "Requriements Sources" page
Add new column "Standard" for the "Source List" page (Backoffice → Product Requirements → Source) with the data from the corresponding "Standard" field of the Source (string). Remove irrelevant columns "Is Security Level" and "Is Regional".
Add new optional dropdown field "Standard" to the pages "New Source" and "Edit Source", which should allow the user to select standard available in PICASso (active in the Stadards LoV). Remove irrelevant fields "Is Security Level" and "Is Regional".
Frontoffice
Default view of requirements tracebility on Product Details
On the Product Details page the user should see new field "Default Requirements traceability view" on the "Product Configuration sub-tab". This field should allow to select the default perspective to view the data on the Product Requirements page in the releases of this product.
The field is a dropdown field which will have the following options:
All requirements (default option)
All the possible combination of standards mapping (as per the configuration in the Backoffice "Requirements Mapping" page)
E.g., if in the Backoffice we have the following requirements mapping pairs:
Standard
Requirement
Linked Standard
Linked Requirement
CTI/CTS
CTI042B_004
CTI048_002
CTI048_020
CTI048_021
CTI048_031
CTI048_033
IEC62443
CR 1.1
SR 1.1
CTI/CTS
CTS048_005
CTS048_006
IEC62443
CR 1.1
IEC62443
SR 1.1
OWASP
V6.3.4
V6.3.8
CTI/CTS
CTI042B_004
CTI048_002
CTI048_020
CTI048_021
CTI048_031
CTI048_033
OWASP
V6.3.4
V6.3.8
For the field "Default Requirements traceability view" we will have the following options:
All Requirements
CTI/CTS → IEC62443
IEC62443 → CTI/CTS
IEC62443 → OWASP
OWASP → IEC62443
CTI/CTS → OWASP
OWASP → CTI/CTS
Option selected for the field "Default Requirements traceability view" on the product would show the list of Product Requriements in all the releases of this product with this tracebility view already selected.
For all existing products this field should be added with "Default Requirements traceability view" = "All requirements".
Updating buttons to icons on Product Requirements and Process Requirements pages
When user opens Product Requriements and Process requirements page in the release, he should see the buttons and toggles displayed on the page were updated to the icons shown in the right corner over the table header:
Refresh Jama (or Jira) - this icon is shown only when requirements were submitted to Jama/Jira with respective "Last update from Jira <date and time>"/"Last update from Jama <date and time>" message. And respective error icon and hover message (if the data were not updated due to an error).
Diagrams icon - opens the "Requirement Status Summary" pop-up
Settings pop-up - shows toggles available for the page:
Process requirements:
Show Sub-Requriements (only if they are visible on the page) - expand all sub-requirements on the page
Show All Requirements - show all (even not scopped) requirements
Product requirements:
Show Sub-Requriements - expand all sub-requirements on the page
Show All Requirements - show all (even not scopped) requirements
Tracebility view (drop-down) - allow to select the perspective to view the list of the Product Requirements in this release.
Import/Export icon - upon clicking, showing 2 options:
Import XLSX - allowing to import file to update requirements statuses or create new custom requirements (only shown during Scoping, Scope Approval and Manage stages)
Note: import progress and link to the error pop-up would be shown under the icon:
Export XLSX - allowing to export file with the list of requirements, their statuses and delegation details.
Note: for users with view privilege and on the stages where requirement's status cannot be edited, upon clicking on this icon - export of the file should be started.
Selecting/changing Requirements Tracebility view in Release
Upon clicking on the "Options" icon on the Product Requirements page, the user should see dropdown field "Traceability view" where all active standards mapping pairs available in PICASso can be selected:
Upon selecting specific mapping pair from the list, the user should see that requirements list view is refreshed and linked requirements from the selected mapping pair are shown connected on the Product Requirements page in the following hierarchy:
Parent of the requirement from 1st Standard in selected the pair
Requirement linked to the parent requriement from 2nd Standard in selected the pair (if any) - marked in blue with the chain icon in front of it
Sub-requirement related to this parent from 1st Standard in selected the pair
Requirement linked to this sub-requriement from 2nd Standard in selected the pair (if any) - marked in blue with the chain icon in front of it
E.g., If user has selected Tracebility view = "IEC62443 → CTI/CTS", than "IEC62443" is considered 1st standard and "CTI/CTS" - 2nd.
But If user has selected Tracebility view = "CTI/CTS → IEC62443", than "CTI/CTS" is the 1st standard and "IEC62443" - 2nd.
Note: 1) Category of the linked requriement should be ignored: If requirement from the linked standard is related to another category - linked requriement should be still shown under its mapped requirement from the 1st standad.
2) If linked requirement is not scoped for the release - it should be shown on the page, only when "Show All Requirements" toggle is activated or when this requirement is added manually to release.
All requriements not related to selected mapping pair in the "Traceability view" field, should be still shown on the page, using for sorting order numbers available in the Backoffice. "Traceability view" selected should be treated as updated display, not as the filter.
When specific "Traceability view" is selected the user should see "Tracebility focus" filter, which will allow the user to select how they want to see these mapped requriements from 3 options:
All requirements (default) - both mapped and not mapped would be shown for selected mapping pair.
Only gaps (not linked) - only requriements which are not linked using this selected mapping pair would be shown in the list.
Only linked - only requriements which are mapped using this selected mapping pair would be shown in the list.
Adding "Standard" filter for the "Product Requirements" page
The user should see new filter "Standard" on the "Product Requriements" page that should allow the user to see in the list only requirements connected to selected standard. The list for the dropdown should be taken from the active standards available in PICASso (active in the Stadards LoV).
Mapped requirements display for Requirements Evaluation
Do we need this view here?
